title: Close setup timing by attacking critical paths
description: Reduce logic depth on the worst negative-slack paths through pipelining or restructuring before relaxing the clock.
body: Use when STA reports negative setup slack at the target frequency. Do not lower the clock frequency as the first move, since that may mask a fixable logic-depth problem. Actions: inspect the worst-slack paths, count logic levels, and add pipeline registers or restructure the logic to shorten the path. Validation signals include rising worst-slack toward positive after each change and a critical-path logic depth within the achievable budget. Risk boundary: a path that cannot close even after pipelining may need an architecture change, so escalate rather than indefinitely tweaking placement.
